Internal legal memo — Nam Son Materials JSC (NSM) — 30/05/2026 — compliance review of PII under Decree 13 and MNPI controls for planned công bố thông tin to Saigon Equity Exchange (SGE).
Under the Securities Law, MNPI refers to issuer/securities information not yet public with a likely significant price impact; our internal reviewer is the National Securities Bureau (NSB‑VN) liaison per SGE rules (fictional bodies).
NSM is preparing “Project Stonebird” disclosures (Q2 earnings draft and a private placement outline) for SGE; timing and pricing are not yet public and must be handled in line with MNPI policy P‑MNPI‑04.
Mr. Tran Minh Quang (born 14/07/1986), Deputy Finance Manager at NSM, sits at HQ: 10F Northern Park Tower, 88 Phuong Minh Blvd., Ward Thien Phuc, Ho Giang City; contact: quang.tran@nsmaterials.vn and 0907 88 66 45.
Device and accounts linked to him include laptop asset tag NSM‑LT‑07A9; handset “Galaxy A51” IMEI 3581‑00‑**‑**‑9432; Thien Phong Commercial Bank payroll AC***7 4 2 9; VPN session U1‑221‑443 at 21:17 ICT 05/05/2026 (IP 10.4.8.21); Zalo: quangtm86; cookie sid=u1_221_443.
On 05/05 he downloaded “Q2‑2026 draft earnings” and the private placement term sheet (indicative VND 18,500/share, 12.0% allotment to Blue Fern Fund Ltd., fictional) via https://intra.nsmaterials.vn/d/r?sid=U1-221-443&doc=Sto ne bird_v2.pdf and shared a screenshot with CFO Ms. Pham Thu Lan at 21:19; these items are material and non‑public.
He also reviewed the signed amendment with Bao Minh Capital Bank (BMCB) to roll the VND facility from 9.2% to 8.3% with covenant relief through 2027 (Doc: BM C B / A M N D / 2 0 2 6 ‑ 0 3; wire ref TRX‑VN‑2026‑11458; “credit package delta” tab shows forward‑looking ratios).
The draft board minute states there has been no material adverse change and no MAC trigger under the 2024 notes; this sentence is generic commercial language and is not PII.
Per Decree 13, the above cluster is personal data associated with a specific person and must be minimized, access‑controlled, and transferred only as required for NSB‑VN submissions; redact accounts and device IDs in external working papers.
Access log excerpt shows “H o G i a n g ‑ G u e s t ‑ W i f i” SSID, device MAC f0:9a:4c:**:**:2d, and shared drive path \\nsm‑fs01\fi n \q2_draf t; these stand‑alone items are quasi‑identifiers and are sensitive only when linkable to a person.
Separated weak identifiers example: age 34, District 7 of Ho Giang, device tag TB‑0011, and user code u‑1995‑d7 appear in a facilities ticket; without a name or role, these should not identify any individual on their own.
OCR artifacts: “N S M ‑ 2 0 2 6 ‑ 1 1 3   E m p l o y e e   C a r d” and codes 00‑7 3 ‑6 1 with phone ext x 2 3 4 5 were scraped from a scan; treat as low confidence until verified against the HR system.
Market context references (e.g., publicly available provincial mining quota summaries and product names like “StoneMix 50”) are public‑source and not MNPI; do not over‑restrict such materials.
The team is mostly mid‑career finance staff and manufacturing engineers; generic demographics and job categories are not by themselves identifying data.
next steps: legal to send redline by eod, finance to scrub q2 tabs for pii and mnpi markers, it to validate role‑based access on SGE folder; questions to compliance@nsmaterials.vn.
